cspd.name: Catholic Schools Parramatta Diocese

solutions.pain_addressed: Poor and inconsistent student literacy and numeracy outcomes; teachers lacking structured curriculum resources and evidence-based instructional frameworks
solutions.solution: Catalyst program — system-wide explicit instruction rollout (High Impact Teaching Practice / HITP) with structured curriculum resources developed in partnership with Ochre Education and a professional learning program built on the Science of Learning
solutions.detail: Launched 2020 across all 56 schools. 5,400+ professional learning units completed; 5,400+ curriculum resources developed for primary English and Mathematics. Deloitte Access Economics commissioned as external evaluator from inception, publishing findings in 2024. Teachers save up to 6 hours/week through structured resources. 4 in 5 teachers recommend the approach.
solutions.outcome_evidence: 75% of CECG students assessed as proficient readers in 2024 vs national average of 66%. 13 of top 20 ACT schools 'making the most difference' (ACARA 2023/24) were CECG schools. Multiple CECG schools (e.g. Sts Peter and Paul Primary Garran, St Vincent's Primary Aranda) achieving scores above ACT, NSW and national averages.
solutions.implication: CSPD's Uplift framework (launched August 2025) is directly modelled on CECG's Catalyst approach — the new EGM Learning Outcomes Patrick Ellis came from CECG. CECG's 5-year trajectory is the closest available roadmap for what CSPD can expect: professional learning infrastructure investment, curriculum resource production at scale, and external evaluation as a credibility mechanism. The Catalyst timeline suggests CSPD should invest now in structured resources and external evaluation to have measurable outcomes to report by 2027-2028.

solutions.pain_addressed: Inconsistent instructional quality and literacy/numeracy outcomes across a large school network; need for a system-wide pedagogical framework that teachers can practically implement
solutions.solution: Vision for Instruction (VFI) — launched February 2024 across all 291 primary schools; system-wide adoption of explicit instruction for literacy, mathematics, and writing; teacher professional development and curriculum resources aligned to the Science of Learning
solutions.detail: VFI is part of MACS 2030 strategy. Accompanied by Vision for Engagement (managing screen time, student wellbeing) and Vision for Leadership. Rollout was system-directed — not optional. MACS Chief of Education Shauna-Maree Sykes publicly attributed NAPLAN numeracy improvements to VFI.
solutions.outcome_evidence: Schools that were early adopters of explicit instruction (pre-VFI) showed NAPLAN improvements of up to 46 percentage points. MACS reported 'widespread shift in proficiency levels in Year 3, 5, 7 and 9 in numeracy' following VFI rollout across 291 primary schools.
solutions.implication: MACS is one year ahead of CSPD on the same pedagogical transformation. Their early NAPLAN data provides the strongest publicly available benchmark for what CSPD's Uplift framework can achieve by 2026-2027. MACS's companion frameworks (Vision for Engagement on screen time, Vision for Leadership) suggest CSPD will need parallel investments in school leadership capability and student wellbeing infrastructure as Uplift scales.

solutions.pain_addressed: Fragmented, legacy HR and finance systems unable to support a network of ~300 schools; no unified digital infrastructure to enable AI adoption or centralised reporting
solutions.solution: Cloud ERP transformation: SAP S/4HANA Cloud (finance/operations) + SAP SuccessFactors (HR/people) deployed by Infosys across all ~300 schools; unified identity and access management; master data management
solutions.detail: Announced July 2025. Programme designed to give every staff member role-based access to a single digital workspace; enable team-based communities; streamline communication. Explicit AI-readiness framing: 'paves the way to adopt generative AI applications geared towards educational outcomes.' Rollout in waves to allow adoption at school level.
solutions.outcome_evidence: Announced July 2025; implementation ongoing. No published outcome data yet.
solutions.implication: CSPD's CIO role was elevated in November 2023 and the system runs Canvas LMS + Classm8 + Grace intranet across 80+ schools. The MACS ERP investment signals that at CSPD's scale, fragmented finance/HR/people systems are a material risk to operational efficiency and AI readiness. CSPD should assess whether its current back-office infrastructure can support the data integration required for Uplift's formative assessment loop and future AI tools.

solutions.pain_addressed: Teacher burnout and workload — nearly half of Australian educators surveyed in 2023 considered leaving the profession; administrative burden consuming teaching time and contributing to attrition
solutions.solution: Microsoft 365 Copilot deployment to 12,500 educators and support staff — largest K-12 generative AI rollout globally. Custom Copilot Studio solution grounded in Catholic values to guide lesson planning with Catholic lens. Phased rollout Jan 2025 to 2026.
solutions.detail: Trial at St Francis College Crestmead showed average 9.3 hours saved per teacher per week in administrative tasks, information search, and lesson/curriculum planning. BCE also launched a custom AI chatbot across 146 schools — first sector-wide generative AI chatbot in a non-government school system. Students use Copilot to take ownership of their learning.
solutions.outcome_evidence: 9.3 hours/week saved per teacher in trial. 8 pilot schools using AI-assisted teaching grew NAPLAN scores from 490 to 555 (2023 to 2024); Year 3 improved 7.22% while state average fell 0.57%.
solutions.implication: CSPD's 69% employee engagement score and Uplift framework implementation both depend on retaining and energising teachers. BCE's Copilot trial data (9+ hours/week saved) is the most credible published evidence of AI's workload impact in Australian Catholic schools. CSPD currently blocks open generative AI ('domed' approach) — this is appropriate risk management but BCE's custom-grounded model shows a pathway to providing AI tools within a controlled, values-aligned environment. The teacher retention business case for AI tools is now empirically supported.

solutions.pain_addressed: Inability to identify at-risk students early; principals lacking real-time, integrated data to compare school performance, track initiatives, or intervene before students fall behind
solutions.solution: LEADing Lights platform — single Microsoft cloud platform (Dynamics 365, Office 365, Claned) integrating student performance, attendance, wellbeing, HR, and finance data across all 164 schools; principal-level Power BI dashboards with real-time benchmarking against NAPLAN, WACE, system averages and past years
solutions.detail: Platform goal: move from 'big picture analytics' to 'moment in time, individual person recommendations' — flagging unusual absence patterns or unexpected assessment drops for individual students. Three-year Microsoft global partnership for data science expertise. 90,000+ M365 accounts, 1PB+ data. Also includes historical reporting back 15 years. Connectivity investment: dual NBN fibre + 4G into all 164 schools, 400%+ speed increase.
solutions.outcome_evidence: Platform is operational; connectivity investment complete. Predictive individual-student recommendations remain aspirational ('want to move to' framing in 2023 reporting). No published student outcome data attributable directly to LEADing Lights.
solutions.implication: CSPD's Uplift framework relies on formative assessment data — but the system's ability to aggregate and act on that data at scale depends on infrastructure that does not yet appear to exist at the CSPD system level. CSPD's Canvas LMS deployment gives it a data collection layer; what it likely lacks is the analytics and reporting layer that CEWA has built. A CSPD equivalent of LEADing Lights — even a lighter version — would turn Uplift from a school-level practice into a system-level intelligence capability.

solutions.pain_addressed: Fragmented IT service management across 164 geographically dispersed schools; email-based legacy helpdesk unable to support centralised operations after corporate governance restructure in 2020
solutions.solution: Freshservice ITSM platform deployed system-wide — unified incident/change management, self-service portal, automated onboarding/offboarding workflows, asset lifecycle management (via Discovery Agent and Jamf integration for Apple devices), team and leadership dashboards
solutions.detail: Replaced a rigid, email-based ITSM system after CEWA's 2020 governance restructure that centralised operations. Workflow Automator simplified ticket management. Change Advisory Board established. Schools are now requesting their own localised Freshservice implementations.
solutions.outcome_evidence: Improved agent morale; enhanced transparency between IT staff and school principals; increased demand from schools for Freshservice access. No quantified metrics published.
solutions.implication: CSPD underwent a similar governance restructure in January 2023 (new company limited by guarantee, CIO role created November 2023). If CSPD's IT service management is still fragmented across 80+ schools, the CEWA Freshservice model is a directly applicable reference. Low-complexity, high-visibility quick win.

solutions.org_name: Catholic Schools NSW (CSNSW) — all dioceses incl. SCS
solutions.pain_addressed: School leaders and system administrators spending 6-8 weeks annually manually locating and reconciling data across multiple siloed school systems; inability to combine attendance, extracurricular, and academic data for integrated student insights
solutions.solution: NSW Catholic Data and Analytics Platform — Azure Databricks for data ingestion from all schools and dioceses; Power BI for reporting; Analytics Workbench and DataShare for agile analytics; implemented with Microsoft partner Insight
solutions.detail: Serves 257,000 students across 11 NSW dioceses plus 46 independent Catholic schools. Automatically ingests NSW Department of Education census data. Enables per-school dashboards combining attendance, extracurricular and academic data. 'Literally saving hundreds of hours of time.'
solutions.outcome_evidence: 6-8 weeks per year saved in data reconciliation per diocese. Instant access to Department of Education census requirements previously requiring manual compilation.
solutions.implication: CSPD is one of the 11 NSW dioceses served by this platform. To the extent CSPD is already connected to the CSNSW data platform, the marginal investment is adding school-level and student-level analytics on top of the existing foundation. The priority is not building from scratch — it is activating the system-level data CSPD already produces through Canvas LMS and Classm8 and surfacing it in a form principals can act on.

solutions.pain_addressed: School reports that parents describe as uninformative; over-reliance on twice-yearly academic grade summaries that fail to communicate the full picture of student development
solutions.solution: New student assessment and reporting framework being developed with Boston College researchers; measures creativity, discernment, resilience, practical wisdom, and problem-solving alongside academic results; questions whether twice-yearly reporting cycle should continue
solutions.detail: Announced February 2026 by ED Danielle Cronin. Described as part of the eight-year Magis 2033 strategic plan. Framework under development — no implementation date confirmed. NAPLAN and HSC remain relevant but are not the only lens.
solutions.outcome_evidence: Framework still in development as of February 2026.
solutions.implication: CSPD's Five-Year Strategic Plan (2024-2029) includes 'Relevance' as a pillar — student and family experience of education is explicitly a strategic priority. SCS's reporting overhaul signals that the sector is converging on broader, richer parent communication as a differentiation strategy. CSPD has Canvas LMS deployed system-wide, which already generates granular student engagement and assessment data. The infrastructure for more frequent, richer reporting to parents may already exist — the question is whether it is being surfaced effectively.

patterns.label: Explicit instruction as the dominant pedagogical intervention
patterns.detail: Three of five lookalikes (CECG, MACS, BCE) have deployed system-wide explicit instruction frameworks. All three report measurable NAPLAN improvements. The evidence base is now strong enough that this is no longer an experimental approach — it is sector consensus. CSPD's Uplift launch in 2025 is consistent with this pattern but is 1-5 years behind peers. Speed of implementation and quality of curriculum resource production will be the key differentiators.

patterns.label: AI for teacher workload relief — ahead of AI for student learning
patterns.detail: BCE's Copilot deployment is the most advanced example; CECG's curriculum resources also reduce teacher planning time. The primary AI use case being deployed at scale in Catholic systems is administrative and lesson planning burden reduction, not direct student-facing AI. BCE's 9.3 hours/week saving per teacher is the most-cited figure in the sector and creates a benchmark CSPD will be expected to respond to. CSPD's current 'domed' AI policy (blocking open generative AI) is defensible but may create tension as peer systems demonstrate teacher retention benefits.

patterns.label: Cloud data platforms as the precondition for AI and analytics
patterns.detail: CEWA (LEADing Lights), CSNSW (Azure Databricks), and MACS (SAP S/4HANA) have all made large infrastructure investments in unified cloud data platforms before deploying AI or advanced analytics. This is a consistent pattern: the data plumbing has to come first. CSPD's challenge is that Canvas LMS gives it a rich data layer for learning, but back-office (finance, HR, facilities) data likely remains siloed. Without integration, the 'full student view' that CEWA aspires to remains impossible.

patterns.label: External evaluation as a credibility mechanism
patterns.detail: CECG commissioned Deloitte Access Economics as an independent external evaluator from day one of Catalyst. MACS cited independent NAPLAN analysis for VFI outcomes. BCE cited Black Dog Institute research on teacher burnout. Catholic systems are increasingly using external evaluation partnerships to validate strategic investments and build public trust. CSPD endorsed Uplift with AERO CEO Dr Jenny Donovan — this is a solid start, but a longitudinal external evaluation commitment would strengthen credibility.

patterns.label: Workforce retention as a strategic platform investment
patterns.detail: BCE's Copilot deployment is explicitly framed around teacher retention (Black Dog Institute burnout data). CECG's curriculum resources reduce teacher planning load. CEWA's data platform includes staff wellbeing metrics. The sector has moved from treating workforce as an HR problem to treating it as a platform investment problem — the systems that retain teachers are investing in reducing cognitive load and improving working conditions through technology.

gaps: No peer has published a credible solution to the LBOTE student support challenge at scale. CSPD has 53.4% LBOTE students (vs 36% NSW Catholic sector average). CECG, MACS and BCE operate in more homogeneous demographic contexts. This is CSPD's most distinctive structural characteristic and no market solution exists that has been validated at comparable scale.

High-growth greenfield school establishment: CSPD is opening new schools (Sacred Heart Westmead, Term 3 2026) in a rapidly expanding corridor while managing 500+ applicants turned away annually. No peer has documented a technology or operational model for managing rapid school establishment while maintaining system coherence.

Operating deficit management: CSPD's -$20.6m operating deficit (2023) against $857m revenue is a structural financial challenge that none of the researched lookalikes have publicly addressed. No peer has documented technology investments explicitly framed around cost reduction or revenue diversification for Catholic diocesan systems.

Catholic identity formation + technology: CSPD has a dedicated Formation Foundation document and Faith in Action Coordinators in every school. BCE's AI chatbot is grounded in Catholic values but no peer has published a rigorous account of how digital platforms interact with (or undermine) Catholic identity formation. This is an unaddressed design challenge for CSPD's AI policy evolution.

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander student engagement: CSPD's fee waiver for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families from 2026 and Innovate RAP 2025-2027 are forward-looking. No peer in this study has documented a technology-enabled model for supporting First Nations students in a predominantly suburban Catholic school context.

acasus.name: Acasus

solutions.pain_addressed: Scaling data capabilities beyond project work
solutions.solution: Embedded data science team as a service line
solutions.detail: IDinsight built a permanent data science practice that serves both internal projects and external clients directly. Positioned as a differentiator vs traditional development consultancies.

solutions.pain_addressed: Revenue concentration on few large donors
solutions.solution: Diversification into commercial advisory
solutions.detail: OPM expanded from pure donor-funded work into commercial government advisory, reducing dependency on DFID/FCDO contracts. Now serves middle-income country governments directly.

solutions.pain_addressed: Scaling delivery without proportional headcount growth
solutions.solution: Technology-enabled program management platform
solutions.detail: Palladium invested in proprietary program management and M&E tooling to standardise delivery across country offices, reducing the consultant-heavy model.

patterns.label: Data/tech as a named service line
patterns.detail: Development consultancies (IDinsight, Palladium, DAI) are all building dedicated data/technology practices rather than treating tech as a project add-on. Acasus's Budapest data team fits this pattern.

patterns.label: Diversifying away from single-donor dependency
patterns.detail: OPM, Dalberg, and R4D have all expanded client base beyond traditional bilateral donors to include foundations, multilateral banks, and commercial government contracts.

gaps: AI-powered policy analysis — no development consultancy has productised AI for policy document analysis or evidence synthesis at scale
Real-time program monitoring using satellite/remote sensing — discussed in the sector but no boutique has operationalised it

phinma.name: PHINMA Education

solutions.pain_addressed: Scaling student support across campuses
solutions.solution: AI-powered adaptive learning platform (Kroton)
solutions.detail: Cogna's Kroton subsidiary deployed adaptive learning technology across its network of 170+ campuses in Brazil, personalising content delivery for low-income students with varied preparation levels.
solutions.source: Cogna annual report and investor presentations

solutions.pain_addressed: Board exam pass rates as competitive differentiator
solutions.solution: Intensive review programs and faculty incentives
solutions.detail: iPeople invested in dedicated board exam review centres and performance-linked faculty compensation to drive engineering and nursing pass rates.

solutions.pain_addressed: Student retention in affordable HE segment
solutions.solution: Predictive analytics for at-risk students
solutions.detail: Deployed machine learning models to identify students likely to drop out based on engagement, grades, and financial signals. Triggers proactive advisor outreach.

patterns.label: Adaptive learning tech for scale
patterns.detail: Affordable HE operators in Brazil (Cogna, Yduqs) and US (Strayer) have all invested in adaptive platforms. PHINMA's scale (177K students) makes this pattern relevant.

patterns.label: Predictive retention models
patterns.detail: At-risk student identification using data is now standard at scale operators. Each 1% retention improvement = ~1,780 students for PHINMA.

gaps: Employer-connected curriculum — no affordable HE operator has built real-time labour market feedback into curriculum design despite employability being the core value proposition
Cross-country student mobility — PHINMA operates in Philippines and Indonesia but has no framework for credit transfer or student exchange between its own institutions

cecg.name: Catholic Education Canberra and Goulburn

solutions.pain_addressed: Inconsistent student literacy and numeracy outcomes; teachers lacking structured curriculum resources and evidence-based instructional frameworks
solutions.solution: Uplift programme — system-wide explicit instruction rollout modelled directly on CECG's Catalyst. Launched August 2025. Patrick Ellis (former CECG Catalyst programme lead) appointed EGM Learning Outcomes to lead it. Uses same partners: Ochre Education (curriculum resources) and Shaping Minds (instructional coaching).
solutions.detail: Uplift was designed after Patrick Ellis transferred from CECG to CSPD. The programme replicates Catalyst's three-pillar structure (curriculum resources, High Impact Teaching Practice, evidence-based assessment). CSPD serves ~40,000 students across ~80 schools in western Sydney — a denser urban context than CECG's ACT/regional NSW footprint.
solutions.implication: CECG is the originator of this model. CSPD's adoption is a validation signal, not a learning opportunity. CECG should position Catalyst as the national template — and track CSPD's Uplift results to demonstrate that the model transfers across different demographics. Any vendor selling tools to support Uplift at CSPD should approach CECG first as the five-year-ahead version of the same deployment.

solutions.pain_addressed: Inconsistent instructional quality and literacy/numeracy outcomes across a large school network; need for a system-wide pedagogical framework based on the Science of Learning
solutions.solution: Vision for Instruction (VFI) — launched February 2024, system-wide adoption of explicit instruction for literacy, mathematics, and writing across all 291 primary schools. Accompanied by curriculum resources and teacher professional development.
solutions.detail: VFI is MACS's analogue to Catalyst, launched 4 years after CECG. System-directed rollout across ~300 schools. Uses Ochre Education mathematics units and Spelling Mastery (same as CECG). Accompanied by Vision for Engagement (screen time, wellbeing) and Vision for Leadership (school leadership capability). Chief of Education Shauna-Maree Sykes publicly attributed NAPLAN numeracy improvements to VFI.
solutions.implication: MACS's early 2024-2025 NAPLAN data (up to 46 percentage point improvements at early adopter schools; 13.6% increase in numeracy proficiency, 17.6% increase in reading proficiency at one reported school) provides a cross-system benchmark for what explicit instruction delivers. CECG already has 5 years of this data — MACS's results reinforce the case. MACS's companion Vision for Leadership framework suggests CECG should assess whether school leadership capability is a constraint on Catalyst deepening — a potential coaching or leadership development opportunity.

solutions.pain_addressed: Fragmented HR, finance, and operations systems unable to support a large multi-site school network; no unified digital infrastructure to enable AI adoption or centralised reporting
solutions.solution: SAP S/4HANA Cloud (finance/operations) + SAP SuccessFactors (HR/people) ERP transformation delivered by Infosys across all ~300 schools. Announced July 2025.
solutions.detail: Consolidates HR, finance, and operations onto a single cloud platform. Explicit AI-readiness framing: 'paves the way to adopt generative AI applications geared towards educational outcomes.' Role-based access for all staff; streamlined communication and team communities. Wave-based rollout to allow school-level adoption.
solutions.implication: CECG's 2026 fee communications cite ICT as a rising cost driver, and the new ED is emphasising system-wide procurement efficiencies. CECG's current technology footprint (Microsoft Teams for Virtual Learning, Catalyst digital resource library) is modest relative to its peer systems. If CECG lacks unified back-office infrastructure, any AI adoption (signalled as a 2025 System Day discussion) will be constrained. The MACS investment signals that at CECG's scale, back-office modernisation is a prerequisite for AI readiness — a potential gap worth assessing.

solutions.pain_addressed: Teacher workload and time-on-task: educators spending excessive hours on administrative, planning, and marking tasks instead of teaching
solutions.solution: Microsoft 365 Copilot deployment to 12,500 educators and support staff — largest K-12 generative AI deployment globally. Phased Jan 2025 to 2026. Grounded in a custom Catholic values chatbot (Copilot Studio).
solutions.detail: Trial at St Francis College Crestmead showed average 9.3 hours saved per teacher per week. BCE developed a custom Copilot grounded in Catholic values for system-wide deployment across 146 schools. This is the first sector-wide generative AI deployment in a non-government school system in QLD/NSW.
solutions.implication: CECG's Catalyst already saves teachers up to 6 hours/week via structured curriculum resources — a comparable time-saving proposition. BCE's Copilot adds another 9.3 hours/week on top of this. CECG has discussed AI in classrooms at the 2025 System Day but has not announced deployment. The BCE model suggests that the next productivity lever for CECG is generative AI tooling layered on top of the Catalyst resource library — not instead of it. Relevant to any vendor proposing AI tools to CECG.

solutions.pain_addressed: Demonstrating measurable academic impact of AI-assisted teaching to school leadership and community
solutions.solution: AI-assisted teaching pilot across 8 schools — combining explicit instruction methodology with AI tools. Measured via NAPLAN scores before and after.
solutions.detail: 8 pilot schools grew NAPLAN scores from 490 to 555 points (2023 to 2024). Year 3 improved 7.22% while the Queensland state average fell 0.57%. Results published publicly and cited by CathNews. BCE Education Strategy 2025-2027 explicitly frames technology and evidence-based teaching as dual pillars.
solutions.implication: CECG's NAPLAN improvement story (largest positive shift of any large diocese 2019-2022) was achieved through pedagogy alone. The BCE evidence suggests that AI tools layered on top of evidence-based teaching can produce incremental NAPLAN gains even against adverse state trends. This provides a data-driven rationale for CECG's new ED David de Carvalho (former ACARA CEO, highly data-oriented) to invest in AI pedagogy tools — with BCE as the benchmark for what returns to expect.

solutions.pain_addressed: Fragmented school IT service management and asset tracking across 164 geographically dispersed schools; low adoption of ITSM by staff
solutions.solution: Freshservice ITSM platform replacing legacy email-based system. Unified asset tracking via Discovery Agent + Jamf integration. Self-service Knowledge Base for onboarding/offboarding. Team-specific dashboards tracking SLAs across all schools.
solutions.detail: After restructuring under corporate governance in 2020, CEWA needed to centrally manage 164 schools. Workflow Automator reduced manual ticket management; Freshservice provided Discovery Agent and Jamf integration for asset lifecycle visibility across 75,000+ students. Member schools requested the same platform for their own localised IT needs.
solutions.implication: CECG incorporated as CACGEL in January 2025 — new corporate governance structure. ICT is cited as a rising cost driver in CECG's 2026 fee communications. If CECG's ITSM infrastructure is still managed school-by-school rather than centrally, the CEWA model offers a directly applicable solution path. Relevant for any vendor proposing centralised IT service management or asset tracking to CECG.

solutions.pain_addressed: Inability to use student performance, attendance, and wellbeing data in real time to inform school leadership decisions and benchmark against system averages
solutions.solution: LEADing Lights data platform — integrated Microsoft cloud ecosystem (Teams, Class Notebook, Office 365, Azure/Claned) enabling real-time analytics for principals: student performance, attendance, graduation rates, and wellbeing benchmarked against school, system, and NAPLAN averages.
solutions.detail: Platform described as 'end to end digital platform for all Catholic schools in WA, encompassing communication, collaboration, finance, administration, data storage, advanced analytics, reporting and more.' Principals can investigate impact of new initiatives via live analytics. 90,000+ Microsoft 365 users across 164 schools; 1+ PB of data.
solutions.implication: CECG's Catalyst uses DIBELS, PAT, and Phonics Screening Check for evidence-based assessment — but the data infrastructure connecting these assessments to principal decision-making is not publicly described. CEWA's LEADing Lights demonstrates what a mature Catholic system analytics layer looks like. The new ED David de Carvalho (former ACARA CEO) is deeply quantitative — a unified analytics dashboard is consistent with his stated emphasis on measurable outcomes. Potential gap for a data/analytics vendor.

solutions.pain_addressed: Inconsistent literacy and numeracy outcomes; teachers lacking evidence-based instructional frameworks in a smaller, geographically dispersed system
solutions.solution: Insight into Learning programme — system-wide explicit instruction and Science of Reading/Learning implementation, modelled on CECG's Catalyst after a 2022 delegation visit.
solutions.detail: CET leaders visited CECG schools in 2022 and returned to build Insight into Learning on the same foundations. PAT assessment data (2022-2023): schools implementing Insight outperformed non-participating schools by up to 11% across mathematics, reading, spelling, and vocabulary. All-System Day 2025 continued professional learning investment. Co-hosted National Science of Learning Summit with CECG.
solutions.implication: CET's adoption of Catalyst as a model is the second direct validation signal (after CSPD). CECG now has two systems (CSPD and CET) plus 30 NZ school leaders who visited in 2025 all referencing Catalyst as the model. This positions CECG as a genuine national thought-leader and exportable model — not just a high-performing local system. Any knowledge-sharing, licensing, or consulting commercialisation of the Catalyst framework could be a revenue opportunity.

solutions.pain_addressed: Insufficient school capacity in highest-growth corridors; ageing facilities unable to support growing enrolments and modern pedagogical approaches
solutions.solution: $300 million capital investment programme — significant upgrades to multiple schools across Central Coast and northern Sydney; precinct model restructuring around K-12 pathway hubs.
solutions.detail: CSBB is the fastest-growing Catholic school system in NSW. The 'Shaping Tomorrow, Together in Faith' strategy (July 2025) establishes six geographic precincts with K-12 pathways, expanded ECE, modern learning spaces, and career pathway linkages. New base campus for Eileen O'Connor Catholic School; master planning for Cathedral College Wahroonga K-12 hub.
solutions.implication: CECG faces a structural variant of the same challenge in different corridors: growth in Gungahlin/Molonglo Valley, decline in Tuggeranong. CSBB's precinct model (hub schools with K-12 pathways) offers a structural template for CECG's own geographic planning. CECG's specific threat is Stromlo Forest Anglican College opening in Molonglo Valley in 2026 — the response may require a comparable capital commitment in that corridor. Relevant for any vendor proposing facilities planning, master planning, or capacity modelling services.

patterns.label: System-wide explicit instruction is the new Catholic sector standard
patterns.detail: Four of six lookalikes (MACS, CET, CSPD, and via CECG itself) have now implemented or are implementing system-wide explicit instruction programmes. MACS (VFI, 2024), CET (Insight into Learning, ~2022-2023), and CSPD (Uplift, 2025) all launched after observing or drawing on CECG's Catalyst (2020). CECG is the originator. The question for CECG is no longer whether to do explicit instruction — it is how to deepen and extend it, and what the next frontier is (AI tools, secondary extension, coaching infrastructure).

patterns.label: Generative AI for teacher productivity is the emerging next wave
patterns.detail: BCE has deployed Microsoft 365 Copilot to 12,500 educators (largest K-12 deployment globally) with 9.3 hours/week saved per teacher and measurable NAPLAN gains in AI-supported schools. CECG has discussed AI at the 2025 System Day but has not announced deployment. MACS's ERP transformation explicitly frames AI readiness as a goal. The gap between CECG and the leading Catholic systems on AI is approximately 1-2 years.

patterns.label: Data infrastructure investment follows pedagogy reform
patterns.detail: CEWA (LEADing Lights), MACS (SAP ERP + analytics), and the CSNSW joint Azure Databricks/Power BI platform for NSW dioceses all represent the second-wave investment after pedagogy: building the data infrastructure to measure, benchmark, and accelerate the pedagogical transformation. CECG's Catalyst evidence base (Deloitte evaluation, DIBELS, PAT) is strong in terms of methodology but the technology platform enabling real-time analytics for 59 schools is not publicly described.

patterns.label: Capital investment in growth corridors is a competitive necessity
patterns.detail: CSBB is investing $300 million in facilities to defend and grow enrolments in NSW growth corridors. In the ACT, CECG's threat is more targeted: Stromlo Forest Anglican College opening in Molonglo Valley in 2026. Growth corridor competition requires proactive capital response — not just pedagogical differentiation.

gaps: No peer has publicly described a robust approach to allied health (speech therapy, OT) access for regional/remote students — a documented CECG challenge. This remains an unresolved gap across the sector.

No Catholic systemic peer has published a detailed model for Virtual Learning that matches CECG's Virtual Learning Collaborative (Year 11-12 access to 12 senior courses via Teams for rural/regional students). CECG may be a leader in this space but limited public benchmarking data is available.

Teacher salary competition with government schools is a documented CECG challenge but no Catholic system has published a structural solution beyond incremental pay increases and non-salary attraction (professional learning, permanency). This remains an unresolved sector-wide problem.

Transition from Catholic primary to Catholic secondary (Year 6 to Year 7) in the ACT appears to be a leakage point to independent schools (CGS, Radford, CGGS) for aspirational families — no peer has published a solution to this specific transition dynamic.
